response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist with roughly 15 years of production software experience, staff-level titles, strong TypeScript/Node.js skills, and recent hands-on work with MCP code generators and agentic-workflow infrastructure, which maps directly to a key technology in this role. However, the AI-specific depth falls well short of the requirement: only about a year of GenAI-adjacent work, with no demonstrated experience in RAG architectures, vector databases, evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks, and no evidence of leading customer-facing technical discovery. Worth a conversation only if the pipeline is thin or the MCP work proves deeper than the resume suggests.
strengths: Recent hands-on MCP and agentic-workflow infrastructure experience at re:cinq
15+ years of production engineering across web, backend, embedded, and high-throughput systems
Strong craftsmanship signals: TDD, CI/CD, workshops, open-source projects, and mentoring
concerns: Falls far short of 8+ years building AI-driven solutions (~1 year of GenAI-adjacent work)
No demonstrated RAG, vector database, evaluation-pipeline, or multi-agent framework experience
No evidence of customer-facing technical discovery or forward-deployed/consulting delivery
